Title: Efficient, cell-type-specific production of flavonols by multiplexed CRISPR activation of a suite of metabolic enzymes

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: root rhizosphere interaction; nutrient uptake; plant defense; human nutrition
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots; strong signal in mature endodermal cells, vasculature/pericycle, young lateral roots
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seedlings grown 5 days on half-strength MS agar at 21 °C under constant light
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful positive-control regulator to induce flavonol pathway, but less precise than enzyme stacking because TF expression triggers broader transcriptome changes
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: myb12 mutant analysis; qRT-PCR of pathway genes; pPER03::MYB12 expression in myb12; DPBA flavonol imaging
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: root-expressed master regulator of flavonol biosynthetic genes; flavonol production in roots largely depends on MYB12
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes root flavonol biosynthesis; loss of MYB12 causes strong reduction of pathway gene expression and near absence of kaempferol/quercetin in roots; endodermal MYB12 expression restores flavonol accumulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: roots; functional in endodermis when expressed from PER03
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly as a regulatory benchmark/positive control. The paper emphasizes enzyme-suite activation as a more precise strategy than TF overexpression. pPER03::MYB12 caused delayed endodermal accumulation and broader flavonol accumulation in meristems, suggesting metabolite transport
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Root flavonol production largely depends on MYB12. 2. myb12 strongly reduces expression of 4CL3, CHS, CHI, FLS1, TT7 and partially F3H, causing near loss of kaempferol and quercetin in roots. 3. Endodermis-specific MYB12 expression rescues flavonol accumulation but with broader effects than targeted enzyme activation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: root rhizosphere interaction; nutrient uptake; plant defense; human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots, especially endodermis after CRISPR activation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old roots; stable cell-type-specific CRISPR activation in myb12; constant light
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Part of a validated six-gene endogenous module for precise flavonol engineering in selected root cell types via CRISPRa
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 4CL3 is MYB12-dependent in roots and had to be included among activated genes to reconstruct pathway output in myb12 endodermis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: entry from phenylpropanoid into flavonoid/flavonol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL3 expression is reduced in myb12 roots. 2. Validated gRNAs enabled endodermal activation of 4CL3. 3. Co-activation with CHS, CHI, F3H, FLS1 and TT7 restored wild-type-level flavonol production in myb12 endodermis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required component promoting flavonol accumulation when co-activated with the full enzyme suite
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream/committed entry into flavonoid branch from phenylpropanoid intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable CRISPRa with validated gRNAs; promoter-reporter activation assay; qRT-PCR; DPBA flavonol imaging
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in root endodermis in Col-0; no longer expressed there in myb12
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: activated in root endodermis by CRISPRa in myb12
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: activates p-coumarate to p-coumaroyl-CoA using CoA and ATP
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies immediate precursor pool for flavonol branch

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: root rhizosphere interaction; nutrient uptake; plant defense; human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots, especially endodermis after CRISPR activation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old roots; stable cell-type-specific CRISPR activation in myb12; constant light
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core enzyme in validated endogenous flavonol engineering cassette
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as having a substrate competitor in lignin pathway; access to substrate may be influenced by metabolic channeling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS expression is strongly reduced in myb12 roots. 2. Validated gRNAs enabled endodermal CRISPRa of CHS. 3. Together with the other five enzymes, CHS activation was required to restore flavonol production in myb12.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for kaempferol/quercetin accumulation when co-activated with other pathway enzymes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable CRISPRa with validated gRNAs; promoter-reporter activation assay; qRT-PCR; DPBA flavonol imaging
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: root expression strongly reduced in myb12
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: activated in root endodermis by CRISPRa in myb12
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form naringenin chalcone
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms early core precursor of flavonols

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: root rhizosphere interaction; nutrient uptake; plant defense; human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots, especially endodermis after CRISPR activation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old roots; stable cell-type-specific CRISPR activation in myb12; constant light
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Part of multiplexed enzyme stack enabling precise local flavonol production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in all successful reconstitution lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHI expression is reduced in myb12 roots. 2. Guide RNAs were individually validated in stable lines. 3. CHI co-activation with the other pathway enzymes enabled endodermal flavonol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for flavonol biosynthesis in reconstructed endodermal pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable CRISPRa with validated gRNAs; promoter-reporter activation assay; qRT-PCR; DPBA flavonol imaging
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: activated in root endodermis by CRISPRa in myb12
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms central intermediate precursor for flavonol branch

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: root rhizosphere interaction; nutrient uptake; plant defense; human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots, especially endodermis after CRISPR activation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old roots; stable cell-type-specific CRISPR activation in myb12; constant light
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Included in validated six-gene module for local flavonol production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYB12 only partially regulates F3H according to qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F3H is partially MYB12-regulated in roots. 2. Stable-guide validation identified working gRNAs. 3. F3H co-activation contributed to endodermal restoration of flavonol accumulation in myb12.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports formation of dihydroflavonol precursors required for kaempferol and quercetin production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable CRISPRa with validated gRNAs; promoter-reporter activation assay; qRT-PCR; DPBA flavonol imaging
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: activated in root endodermis by CRISPRa in myb12
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor of FLS1-derived kaempferol

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: root rhizosphere interaction; nutrient uptake; plant defense; human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots; strong abundance in mature endodermis; endodermal flavonol accumulation after CRISPRa
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old roots; stable cell-type-specific CRISPR activation in myb12; constant light
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Terminal flavonol-forming enzyme useful for engineering kaempferol production and, with TT7, quercetin production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strong endogenous abundance in mature endodermis corroborated flavonol presence there
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLS1 expression is strongly reduced in myb12 roots. 2. Validated CRISPRa restored FLS1 expression in endodermis. 3. FLS1 abundance in mature endodermis correlates with local flavonol accumulation. 4. Co-activation with upstream genes restored wild-type flavonol levels.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly promotes kaempferol formation and contributes to total flavonol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable CRISPRa with validated gRNAs; promoter-reporter activation assay; qRT-PCR; DPBA flavonol imaging
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: reduced in myb12 roots; strong abundance in mature endodermis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: activated in root endodermis by CRISPRa in myb12
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts dihydroflavonols into flavonols such as kaempferol

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: root rhizosphere interaction; nutrient uptake; plant defense; human nutrition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots, especially endodermis after CRISPR activation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 5-day-old roots; stable cell-type-specific CRISPR activation in myb12; constant light
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Without TT7, pathway output shifts to kaempferol-only accumulation rather than quercetin
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Branch-switch enzyme to control kaempferol versus quercetin composition by inclusion/exclusion from CRISPRa gene stack
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: tt7 mutant lacks quercetin and over-accumulates kaempferol; CRISPRa lines lacking TT7 accumulate only kaempferol, while inclusion of TT7 promotes strong quercetin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT7 is strongly reduced in myb12 roots. 2. tt7 mutant validates TT7 as required for quercetin formation and causes kaempferol overaccumulation. 3. In CRISPRa stacks, excluding TT7 yields kaempferol-only accumulation, while including TT7 produces strong quercetin accumulation in endodermis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redirects flavonol pathway toward quercetin production; absence shifts flux to kaempferol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: stable CRISPRa with validated gRNAs; promoter-reporter activation assay; qRT-PCR; DPBA flavonol imaging; tt7 mutant comparison
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: kaempferol precursor (e.g., dihydrokaempferol or naringenin-related intermediate)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: activated in root endodermis by CRISPRa in myb12
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: introduces a 3'-hydroxyl group into the B-ring of flavonoid intermediates, enabling quercetin biosynthesis
